title: Simplify embeddings: use resolveModelFile + switch to EmbeddingGemma-300M
description:
## Problem

The current embedding setup in `src/embeddings/download.ts` is heavyweight compared to OpenClaw's approach:

1. **~90 lines of custom download code** for:
   - Progress reporting
   - Temp file handling with atomic rename
   - Stream backpressure management
   - Manual HuggingFace URL construction

2. **Large model** - nomic-embed-text-v1.5 is ~500MB, heavier than needed for memory search

3. **No fallback options** - if local embedding fails, the system is stuck

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Replace custom download with node-llama-cpp's built-in `resolveModelFile()`:

```typescript
// Before (download.ts + nomic.ts = ~190 lines)
export const MODEL_URL = 'https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/...';
await downloadFile(MODEL_URL, modelPath);
const model = await llama.loadModel({ modelPath });

// After (~10 lines)
import { resolveModelFile } from 'node-llama-cpp';
const MODEL = 'hf:ggml-org/embeddinggemma-300M-GGUF/embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf';
const resolved = await resolveModelFile(MODEL);
const model = await llama.loadModel({ modelPath: resolved });
```

### 2. Switch from nomic-embed-text-v1.5 to EmbeddingGemma-300M:

| Model | Size | Dimensions | Use Case |
|-------|------|------------|----------|
| nomic-embed-text-v1.5.Q4_K_M | ~500MB | 768 | Higher quality, heavier |
| **embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0** | ~300MB | varies | Lighter, good for memory search |

## Benefits

- **Delete ~90 lines** of download infrastructure
- **~40% smaller model** (~300MB vs ~500MB)
- **Built-in caching** handled by node-llama-cpp
- **Simpler API** - just use `hf:` prefix for HuggingFace models
- **Faster cold start** - smaller model loads faster

## Reference

OpenClaw's implementation: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/memory/embeddings.ts

## Scope

- Remove `src/embeddings/download.ts` (or reduce to minimal re-export)
- Rename/update `src/embeddings/nomic.ts` to use `resolveModelFile` + new model
- **Update vector dimension constants** if EmbeddingGemma uses different dimensions
- **Rebuild any existing SQLite indexes** (embedding dimensions may change)
- Update tests
- Update CLI `download-model` command (may become optional/deprecated)
- Update documentation referencing nomic model

## Migration Note

Switching models means existing embeddings in SQLite will be incompatible. Users will need to rebuild their lesson index.
close_reason: Implemented: deleted ~460 lines of custom download code, switched to EmbeddingGemma-300M Q4_0 (~150MB vs ~500MB)

title: Make SQLite cache lossless for unified Lesson optional fields
description:
Context: Lesson schema is now unified with optional evidence/severity. SQLite is a cache rebuilt from JSONL and should preserve data without mutation. Currently rebuildIndex drops evidence/severity for type=quick, and rowToLesson defaults evidence='' and severity='medium' for full lessons, which mutates data and can affect ranking/clients.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] In rebuildIndex(), store evidence and severity for all lessons (no type gating).
- [ ] rowToLesson maps NULL -> undefined (do not default evidence to '' or severity to 'medium').
- [ ] Update any related SQL inserts/reads to allow NULL values without mutation.
- [ ] Add sqlite.test.ts coverage for roundtrip: JSONL -> rebuildIndex -> searchKeyword/read, verifying evidence/severity preserved for: quick with evidence/severity, full without them, and full with them.
- [ ] Ensure ranking behavior still defaults to medium when severity is undefined (severityBoost already handles undefined).

Files: src/storage/sqlite.ts, src/storage/sqlite.test.ts (maybe src/search/ranking.ts only if needed).
close_reason: Fixed lossless roundtrip: store evidence/severity for all lessons, map NULL to undefined

title: Remove legacy QuickLesson/FullLesson exports and update references
description:
Context: Repo is not deployed, so we can remove legacy QuickLesson/FullLesson types and schemas instead of carrying deprecated API. This simplifies types and avoids refine-based schemas that no longer provide strong typing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Remove QuickLessonSchema and FullLessonSchema exports from src/types.ts and src/index.ts.
- [ ] Remove QuickLesson/FullLesson type aliases from src/types.ts and re-export list in src/index.ts.
- [ ] Update all internal usages to rely on Lesson only (CLI, retrieval, storage, tests, examples).
- [ ] Replace any type guards relying on FullLesson alias with inline checks (lesson.type === 'full') and handle optional fields explicitly.
- [ ] Update docs and references (doc/PLAN.md, doc/standards/typescript-best-practices.md, doc/invariants/type-unification.md) to reflect removal.
- [ ] Tests updated accordingly; no unused imports remain.

Files: src/types.ts, src/index.ts, src/cli.ts, examples/*, doc/*, src/**/*.test.ts, src/retrieval/session.ts, src/search/ranking.test.ts, src/storage/*.test.ts, src/capture/quality.test.ts, src/search/vector.test.ts.
close_reason: Implemented in parallel: import CLI, retrieval count tracking, legacy type removal

title: Write detailed packaging spec for pnpm distribution
description:
Goal: Document a meticulous, implementation-ready plan to ship Learning Agent as a pnpm package (library + CLI) for developer laptops. This doc should guide packaging, publishing, and user install flows without Docker.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Create doc/specs/packaging.md using the spec template.
- [ ] Covers goals, non-goals, constraints (Node >=20, native deps), and target users.
- [ ] Documents install flows: `pnpm add -D learning-agent`, `pnpm dlx learning-agent <cmd>`, and local usage.
- [ ] Documents CLI entrypoints, bin names, and required build output (`dist/`).
- [ ] Explicitly states model download behavior (manual `learning-agent download-model`, no postinstall).
- [ ] Enumerates package contents (`files`/`exports`/types) and how they map to build artifacts.
- [ ] Defines publish checklist: build, tests, lint, `pnpm pack` verification, `pnpm publish --dry-run`.
- [ ] Mentions versioning, CHANGELOG updates, and backward compatibility expectations.

Files: doc/specs/packaging.md (new), optionally update doc/PLAN.md or CONTRIBUTING.md with a short pointer to the spec.
close_reason: Packaging spec approved. All requirements validated against package.json.

title: Implement pnpm packaging plan (CLI + publish readiness)
description:
Context: After spec is approved, implement the packaging plan for pnpm distribution.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Validate package.json fields for publish: name, version, bin, exports, main/types, files, engines, license, repository (if applicable).
- [ ] Ensure CLI build output has executable shebang and works via `pnpm dlx learning-agent --help`.
- [ ] Add/confirm README install + usage sections are aligned with spec.
- [ ] Add a lightweight `pnpm pack`/`pnpm publish --dry-run` verification step in CONTRIBUTING or release docs.
- [ ] No postinstall scripts that download large models.
- [ ] All tests and lint pass.

Files: package.json, src/cli.ts (if shebang/build needed), README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, doc/PLAN.md (as needed).
close_reason: Packaging complete: repository field, LICENSE, CHANGELOG in tarball, publish checklist in CONTRIBUTING.md

title: Update CLAUDE.md with mandatory closed-loop TDD
description:
Strengthen TDD enforcement in .claude/CLAUDE.md:

## Make Subagent Workflow Mandatory:
1. /invariant-designer BEFORE any implementation
2. /test-first-enforcer BEFORE implementing
3. /property-test-generator for edge cases
4. /anti-cargo-cult-reviewer during test review
5. /module-boundary-reviewer after implementation
6. /implementation-reviewer as FINAL gate (work NOT complete until APPROVED)

## Add Explicit Exit Criteria:
All 6 categories must pass:
1. Tests: 100% pass, no skipped
2. Regressions: All previous tests pass
3. Code Quality: pnpm lint passes
4. Standards: Type hints, JSDoc on public APIs
5. Bugs: Logic reviewed, edge cases handled
6. Specification: Requirements fulfilled

## Add Closed-Loop Diagram:
Document the iterative review loop - if rejected, fix ALL issues and resubmit
close_reason: Closed

title: Document resource lifecycle (closeDb, unloadEmbedding)
description:
**Problem**: Agents don't know when to call cleanup functions.

**Issues**:
- First embedding call loads 500MB into memory silently
- Database remains open until explicit closeDb()
- Memory leaks if agent doesn't clean up

**Fix**: Add clear JSDoc explaining:
1. When model loads
2. When to call unloadEmbedding()
3. When to call closeDb()
4. Best practices for long-running processes

**Ref**: Codebase detective analysis

title: Document invariants for core modules
description:
Create invariant documentation following Lamport's safety/liveness framework:

## Modules to Document:
1. storage/jsonl - Data invariants for lesson structure
2. storage/sqlite - Index consistency invariants
3. embeddings/nomic - Embedding vector properties
4. search/vector - Search result ordering guarantees
5. capture/quality - Quality score boundaries

## Per Module:
- Data invariants: What must always be true about data
- Safety properties: What must NEVER happen
- Liveness properties: What must EVENTUALLY happen

Store in doc/invariants/ or inline in module READMEs.

Reference: doc/verification/closed-loop-review-process.md for format

title: Document cmake-js override workaround in README
description:
Add a note to README about the deprecated dependency warnings and the pnpm override workaround.

**Add to README**:
If you see deprecation warnings during install, add to your package.json:
```json
{"pnpm": {"overrides": {"cmake-js": "^8.0.0"}}}
```
This is a transitive dependency issue that will be resolved when node-llama-cpp updates.

title: CRITICAL: Add embedding cache in SQLite
description:
**Problem**: Vector search recomputes embeddings for ALL lessons on EVERY search - O(N) expensive operations.

**Location**: src/search/vector.ts:60-66

**Solution**: Cache embeddings in SQLite using the existing `embedding BLOB` column (already defined in schema but never populated).

**Implementation**:
1. Compute embedding when lesson is appended/indexed
2. Store in SQLite `embedding` column as binary blob
3. At search time, load pre-computed vectors instead of recomputing
4. Add content hash to detect when re-embedding is needed

**Impact**: Search goes from O(N * embedding_time) to O(N * cosine_similarity) - 100x faster.

**Ref**: Code analysis review
close_reason: Implemented with TDD - all tests pass

title: [EPIC] Week 3: Polish + Production
close_reason: Epic complete: core features shipped, v0.1.0 published to npm. Hook commands tracked as P1 issues.

title: Deprecated dependencies from node-llama-cpp → cmake-js
description:
**Problem**: Installing learning-agent shows deprecation warnings for are-we-there-yet, gauge, npmlog, and tar.

**Root Cause**: node-llama-cpp@3.15.1 depends on cmake-js@^7.4.0, which uses:
- tar@^6.2.0 (deprecated)
- npmlog@^6.0.2 (deprecated, pulls in are-we-there-yet and gauge)

**Solution**: cmake-js@8.0.0 fixes this by:
- Upgrading tar to ^7.5.6
- Removing npmlog entirely

**Workaround for consumers**: Add to package.json:
```json
{"pnpm": {"overrides": {"cmake-js": "^8.0.0"}}}
```

**Upstream fix needed**: node-llama-cpp should update cmake-js to ^8.0.0

**Tasks**:
1. Open issue in withcatai/node-llama-cpp requesting cmake-js update
2. Document workaround in README
3. Close this issue when upstream fixes it

title: Remove redundant rebuildIndex in isNovel
description:
**Problem**: `isNovel()` and CLI `search` rebuild the entire SQLite index on every call.

**Impact**: Performance bottleneck. O(N) writes for every read.

**Fix**: Implement smart sync/staleness check.
1. Store `last_synced_mtime` in a metadata table or file.
2. Compare with `fs.stat(JSONL_PATH).mtime`.
3. Only `rebuildIndex` if JSONL is newer than the DB sync time.
4. Also allow explicit force rebuild via CLI flag.

**Files**: `src/storage/sqlite.ts`, `src/capture/quality.ts`, `src/cli.ts`
close_reason: Implemented smart sync with mtime tracking - rebuildIndex now only runs when JSONL changes
